https://prez.dev/description.@value: The Geological Sample Material Type vocabulary is a SKOS-based hierarchical classification system for geological specimens. The scheme provides comprehensive coverage of the earth sciences domain by accommodating both natural and processed materials, terrestrial and extraterrestrial sources, and the practical needs of collection management across diverse geological institutions.

https://prez.dev/provenance.@value: Compiled by the TDWG Mineralogy Extension Task Group for community use and to support the extension release by providing standardized values for Material Entity Type, which is central to the Extension and Compound Specimen Model.

http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#defi.@value: A natural, solid object, derived from a celestial body, that was transported by natural means from the body on which it formed to a region outside the dominant gravitational influence of that body and that later collided with a natural or artificial body larger than itself.

http://purl.org/dc/terms/provenance.@value: Definition derived from Rubin AE, Grossman JN (2014) Meteorite and meteoroid: a new comprehensive definition. Meteorit Planet Sci 45:114–122

https://prez.dev/description.@value: A natural, solid object, derived from a celestial body, that was transported by natural means from the body on which it formed to a region outside the dominant gravitational influence of that body and that later collided with a natural or artificial body larger than itself.
